back_translation: On one day (= one occasion), while He was standing beside the Lake of Gennesaret, the crowd was pressing upon Him to hear the word of God,
He saw two boats moored beside the lake, and the fishermen had disembarked from them and were washing their nets.
He got into one of the boats which belonged to Simon, and asked him to move a little away from the shore. Then He sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
When He had finished speaking, He said to Simon, 'Put out into deep water, and let down your nets to catch fish.'
Simon answered, 'Master (boss/overseer), we have toiled through the whole night and got nothing. But because of Your word, I will let down the nets.'
When they had done so, they caught a very large number of fish, so that their nets began to tear.
So they beckoned with their hands to their partners in the other boat to come and help. They came and helped until both boats were filled, so that the boats began to sink.
When Simon Peter saw this, he fell down at the knees of Jesus, saying, 'Lord, please depart from me, for I am a sinful man.'
For amazement seized him and all who were with him, because of the number of fish they had caught.
And likewise also James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said to Simon, 'Do not fear; from now on you will catch people.'
When they had brought the boats to shore, they left everything and followed Him.

On one day, while He was in one of the towns, behold, there was a man full of leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he bowed down, face to the ground, and begged Him, 'Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.'
He extended His hand and touched him, saying, 'I am willing; be clean.' Immediately the leprosy departed from him.
He instructed him, 'Tell no one at all, but go and show yourself to the priest, and offer the offering for your cleansing as Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.'
But the news about Him spread all the more, and great crowds came together to hear Him and to be healed of their various illnesses.
But He often withdrew to solitary places and prayed.
On one day, while He was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the Law who had come from every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem were sitting there. The power of the Lord was with Him to heal.
Behold, some men were carrying a paralyzed man on a mat. They tried to bring him inside and set him before His presence.

When they could not find a way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him with the small mat through the tiles, down into the midst of the people, right before Jesus.
When He saw their faith, He said, 'Friend, your sins have been forgiven.'
The scribes and Pharisees began thinking in their hearts, 'Who is this that speaks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins except God alone?'
Jesus knew their thoughts, and answered, 'Why do you think these things in your hearts?'
'Which is easier — to say, "Your sins have been forgiven," or to say, "Get up and walk"?'
'But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority to forgive sins on earth' — He said to the paralyzed man, 'I command you: get up, take up your mat, and go home.'
Immediately, he rose up before them all, took up what he had been lying on, and went home, praising God.
Everyone was filled with amazement and praised God. They were filled with awe, saying, 'Today we have seen remarkable things.'
After this, He went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax booth. He said to him, 'Follow Me.'
So he left everything, got up, and followed Him.
Then Levi made a great banquet for Him in his own house. There were many tax collectors and others reclining / eating together with them.
The Pharisees and their scribes grumbled to His disciples, 'Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?'
Jesus answered them, 'Healthy people do not need a doctor, but sick people do.'
'I have not come to call the righteous, but to call sinners to repentance.'
They said to Him, 'The disciples of John fast and pray earnestly regularly, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees, but Yours eat and drink.'
He answered them, 'Can you make the bridegroom's friends fast while the bridegroom is still with them?'
'But those days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them; in those days they will fast.'
He also told them a parable: 'No one tears cloth from a new garment to patch on an old one. If one does so, the new garment will also be torn, and the patch from the new will not match the old.'
'And no one pours new wine into old leather skins. If one does so, the new wine will burst the skins, the wine will spill, and the skins will be ruined.'
'But new wine must be poured into new skins.'
'And no one, after drinking old wine, wants new; for he says, "The old is good."'
